title: Pungent Canadian Tofu Bake
description: A flavorful and aromatic Canadian dish featuring marinated tofu, deglazed fond, and a hint of maple syrup.
instructions: In a shallow dish, mix together flour, panko breadcrumbs, and a pinch of salt.
Cut the tofu into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes and coat with the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat 1 tablespoon of canola oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of canola oil.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, thyme, and rosemary.
Return the tofu to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and the tofu is cooked through.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
